title: Roasted Spicy Middle Eastern Chicken Roast
description: A spicy and creamy Middle Eastern chicken roast dish featuring fine yellow cornmeal, perfect for a solo dinner, and is Whole30 friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, garlic powder, cumin, paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the cornmeal mixture.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 25-30 minutes or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the chicken is roasting, mix the lemon juice with 1 tablespoon of water in a small bowl.
After the chicken has roasted for 20 minutes, brush the lemon juice mixture evenly over the top of the chicken.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
